<!-- Generated by Claude. Verify against code. May be stale. Plan/handoff, not permanent docs. Delete when done. -->

# Compiler pipeline refactor: migrate to a Ruff Python AST IR

## Goal

Replace the string-based code generator with a real Python AST as the IR, using
Ruff's AST types, parser, and code generator. Target pipeline:

```
.hyper source
  → Parse   (existing tokenizer + tree builder → hyper AST)
  → Lower   (hyper AST → Ruff ast::ModModule; parse all Python string fragments)
  → Plugins (transform the Ruff AST directly — params, imports, guards, async…)
  → Print   (source-map-aware printer walks Ruff AST → Python string + source map)
```

This eliminates the `plugins::Context` side-channel and the
"codegen-makes-structural-decisions" coupling: every feature becomes an AST
transform, and the printer is mechanical.

## Dependencies (done)

Pinned to Ruff tag **`0.15.17`** in `rust/Cargo.toml`:
`ruff_python_ast`, `ruff_python_parser`, `ruff_python_codegen`,
`ruff_text_size`, `ruff_source_file`. They compile cleanly as git deps.
`tree-sitter`/`tree-sitter-python` are still used by the parser and have **not**
been removed yet (a later cleanup once the parser no longer needs them — note the
tokenizer uses tree-sitter to disambiguate statements, so removal is non-trivial).

## Status

### Done — Phases 1–3 (lowering + all plugins), behind `compile_via_ast`
- **Ruff deps integrated** (`4484605`).
- **`lower` module** (`rust/src/lower/`):
  - `builders.rs` — terse constructors for Ruff nodes. Synthetic nodes get the
    `SENTINEL` range (`0..0`); user-written Python gets a real `TextRange` from
    the hyper `Span` via `span_range`. `is_sentinel`/`SENTINEL` are how the
    Phase-4 printer will decide "does this output map back to `.hyper`?".
  - `mod.rs::lower(ast, function_name) -> ast::ModModule`: partitions top-level
    nodes; user imports; `from hyper import html`; signature with kw-only params
    (and a declared `**name` → the `**kwarg` slot); and the **full body** —
    statements, expressions, all control flow, definitions, fragments, **text /
    elements / components / slots**.
  - `render.rs` — renders a combinable run of text/expression/element nodes into
    Python f-string *source* (mirroring the string generator's content +
    attribute rules), which `lower` parses into a real `ExprFString` /
    `ExprStringLiteral`. Components → `yield from Name(...)` (inner default-slot
    generator for children); slots → `if <slot> is not None: yield from <slot>`
    with the fallback as `else`.
  - `transform.rs` — **all six plugins reimplemented as Ruff-AST passes** (read
    the real lowered code via a ruff `Visitor`, no `Context`):
    `apply_async`, `apply_slots` (+`Iterable` import), `apply_mutable_defaults`
    (guards), `apply_spread_kwargs` (inject/guard `**kwargs`),
    `apply_typing_imports`, `apply_helper_imports`. Reserved-keyword renaming
    still reuses the existing hyper-AST `RenameReservedKeywords` transform (run
    before lowering).
- **`compile_via_ast(source, function_name) -> String`** in `lib.rs` runs the
  whole new pipeline and prints with Ruff's **stock** `Generator`. Output is
  semantically-correct Python but **not yet source-map-aware or byte-identical**.
- A fixture test asserts **every** `.hyper` fixture the string pipeline accepts
  also lowers to valid, placeholder-free Python (re-parsed with
  `ruff_python_parser`). The string pipeline remains the default; suite is green.

### Known gaps before Phase 4/5
- Reserved keyword **attribute/kwarg names** rely on the pre-lowering rename
  pass; verify coverage when wiring as default.
- Whitespace/indentation of combined content is left raw (stock printer); the
  `f"""\…"""` dedented style is Phase 4.

## Remaining work

### Phase 4 — Source-map-aware printer

Vendor `ruff_python_codegen::Generator` (~1500 lines, `crates/
ruff_python_codegen/src/generator.rs`) into the project. It already walks every
node type and writes Python. Extend it:
- track the current output byte offset (the `Output` cursor concept from
  `generate/output.rs`)
- when a node's `TextRange` is non-sentinel (`!is_sentinel`), record a `Range`
  mapping source bytes → compiled bytes
- reproduce hyper's f-string style (`f"""\` triple-quote + backslash
  continuation, content-combined) — this is what makes output byte-identical
- after printing, run the existing `injection_analyzer.rs` logic on the recorded
  ranges to produce `Injection`/`ExpressionBrace`/`TagHighlight` data
- The printer has **zero** Hyper semantics; it only prints Python + records
  positions.

### Phase 5 — Wire up + pass existing expected-output tests

Switch `lib.rs::compile` to Parse → Lower → Plugins → Print. All
`rust/tests/**` expected `.py`/`.json`/`.err` must pass (byte-identical or
re-blessed via `accept_expected` dry-run → review → `--apply`). Source-map data
must still drive the JetBrains plugin.

## Gotchas discovered
- Hyper control-flow header strings **include the trailing block colon**
  (`condition == "x > 1:"`); strip via `header()` before reuse. Mirrors the
  string generator's `.trim_end_matches(':')`.
- Hyper blocks (if/for/while/with/try/match **and** def/class) close with `end`,
  not indentation — relevant when writing `.hyper` test fixtures.
- Lowering reuses Ruff's parser for hard headers via "reconstruct header → parse
  → graft lowered bodies" (see `lower_if`/`lower_for`/…/`lower_definition`).
- Ruff nodes need `node_index: AtomicNodeIndex::NONE` and a `range`; `Suite` is
  `ThinVec<Stmt>` (use `.into_iter().collect()` when assigning a `Vec`).
- `function_name` is the snake_case file stem → PascalCased component name.
